executable.title: DOG_sramtilak
executable.description: Computes the Difference of Gaussians (DOG = G_sigma1(f) - G_sigma2(f)) to enhance edges in a 3D image. Two independent Gaussian low-pass filters with different sigma values are applied and subtracted. The output is rescaled so that the minimum maps to 0 and the maximum maps to 255 (unsigned char). Requires sigma1 != sigma2; larger separation between sigmas increases edge sensitivity at coarser scales.
executable.acknowledgements: ITK RecursiveGaussianImageFilter, SubtractImageFilter, RescaleIntensityImageFilter. ITK Software Guide Book 2, Chapter 2.

parameters.label: Input/Output
parameters.description: Input and output volume file paths

image.label: Input Volume
image.description: 3D input image on which the DOG edge-enhancement filter is applied.

image.label: Output Volume
image.description: Edge-enhanced DOG output image rescaled to unsigned char [0, 255].

parameters.label: Filter Parameters
parameters.description: Sigma values for the two Gaussian kernels. Requires sigma1 != sigma2.

double.label: Sigma 1 (mm)
double.description: Standard deviation of the first (finer-scale) Gaussian kernel. Should be smaller than sigma2. Controls sensitivity to fine edges.

double.label: Sigma 2 (mm)
double.description: Standard deviation of the second (coarser-scale) Gaussian kernel. Should be larger than sigma1. Controls the scale of detected edges.
